context: =  CORTICOTROPHINOMA =  rare cause of Cushing syndrome osteoporosis > purpura > striae > muscle atrophy Prognosis:  almost all malignant with metastases at time of Dx Gastrinoma ◊  2nd most common syndromic pancreatic endocrine tumor (= 50% of insulinoma frequency) Peak age:  5th decade; 8% in patients < 20 years; M÷F = 1.3÷1 Histo:     composed of G cells (in α cells / δ cells) Gastrinoma symptomatology caused by: (a)  islet cell hyperplasia (10%) (b)  benign adenoma (30%): ½ solitary, ½ multiple (especially in MEN 1) (c)  malignancy (50-60%) with metastases to liver, spleen, lymph nodes, bon
